pattern: The best product of three values is either the three largest numbers, or two negatives with the single largest positive, so comparing those two candidates from the sorted extremes suffices.
hints: A pair of large negative numbers multiplies to a large positive, which can beat picking three positives.
You only ever need the three largest and the two smallest values in the array.

edge_inputs.description: all negative, answer is least negative product
two large negatives beat three positives

stress.description: random mix of negatives, zeros and positives across the full value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 10000))
    nums = [rng.randint(-1000, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
